heading: Safe routes to school
label: 208 Safe routes to school

children.heading: .—In the vicinity of schools
children.label: 1 .—In the vicinity of schools
children.text: The term “in the vicinity of schools”, with respect to a school, means the approximately 2-mile area within bicycling and walking distance of the school.

children.heading: .—Primary, middle, and high schools
children.label: 2 .—Primary, middle, and high schools
children.text: The term “primary, middle, and high schools” means schools providing education from kindergarten through 12th grade.

children.heading: .—Establishment
children.label: b .—Establishment
children.text: Subject to the requirements of this section, the Secretary shall establish and carry out a safe routes to school program for the benefit of children in primary, middle, and high schools.
to enable and encourage children, including those with disabilities, to walk and bicycle to school;
to make bicycling and walking to school a safer and more appealing transportation alternative, thereby encouraging a healthy and active lifestyle from an early age; and
to facilitate the planning, development, and implementation of projects and activities that will improve safety and reduce traffic, fuel consumption, and air pollution in the vicinity of schools.

children.label: A
children.text: the total student enrollment in primary, middle, and high schools in each State; bears to

children.label: B
children.text: the total student enrollment in primary, middle, and high schools in all States.

children.heading: .—Minimum apportionment
children.label: 2 .—Minimum apportionment
children.text: No State shall receive an apportionment under this section for a fiscal year of less than $1,000,000.

children.heading: .—Set-aside for administrative expenses
children.label: 3 .—Set-aside for administrative expenses
children.text: Before apportioning under this subsection amounts made available to carry out this section for a fiscal year, the Secretary shall set aside not more than $3,000,000 of those amounts for the administrative expenses of the Secretary in carrying out this section.

children.heading: .—Determination of student enrollments
children.label: 4 .—Determination of student enrollments
children.text: Determinations under this subsection relating to student enrollments shall be made by the Secretary.

children.heading: .—Administration of Amounts
children.label: e .—Administration of Amounts
children.text: Amounts apportioned to a State under this section shall be administered by the State department of transportation.

children.heading: .—Eligible Recipients
children.label: f .—Eligible Recipients
children.text: Amounts apportioned to a State under this section shall be used by the State to provide financial assistance to State, local, Tribal, and regional agencies, including nonprofit organizations, that demonstrate an ability to meet the requirements of this section.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Amounts apportioned to a State under this section may be used for the planning, design, and construction of infrastructure-related projects that will substantially improve the ability of students to walk and bicycle to school, including sidewalk improvements, traffic calming and speed reduction improvements, pedestrian and bicycle crossing improvements, on-street bicycle facilities, off-street bicycle and pedestrian facilities, secure bicycle parking facilities, and traffic diversion improvements in the vicinity of schools.

children.heading: .—Location of projects
children.label: B .—Location of projects
children.text: Infrastructure-related projects under subparagraph (A) may be carried out on any public road or any bicycle or pedestrian pathway or trail in the vicinity of schools.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: In addition to projects described in paragraph (1), amounts apportioned to a State under this section may be used for noninfrastructure-related activities to encourage walking and bicycling to school, including public awareness campaigns and outreach to press and community leaders, traffic education and enforcement in the vicinity of schools, student sessions on bicycle and pedestrian safety, health, and environment, and funding for training, volunteers, and managers of safe routes to school programs.

children.heading: .—Allocation
children.label: B .—Allocation
children.text: Not less than 10 percent and not more than 30 percent of the amount apportioned to a State under this section for a fiscal year shall be used for noninfrastructure-related activities under this paragraph.

children.heading: .—Safe routes to school coordinator
children.label: 3 .—Safe routes to school coordinator
children.text: Each State shall use a sufficient amount of the apportionment of the State for each fiscal year to fund a full-time position of coordinator of the safe routes to school program of the State.

children.label: A
children.text: to operate a national safe routes to school clearinghouse;

children.label: B
children.text: to develop information and educational programs on safe routes to school; and

children.label: C
children.text: to provide technical assistance and disseminate techniques and strategies used for successful safe routes to school programs.

children.heading: .—Funding
children.label: 2 .—Funding
children.text: The Secretary shall carry out this subsection using amounts set aside for administrative expenses under subsection (d)(3).

children.heading: .—Treatment of Projects
children.label: i .—Treatment of Projects
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, a project assisted under this section shall be treated as a project on a Federal-aid highway under chapter 1.
